note_data.title: Graphs
keyPoints: Natural numbers are all the positive integers from 1 to infinity, fractions or decimals are not included, they are also called Positive Integers
Whole Numbers are all the positive integers including zero
Integers include positive numbers, negative numbers or zero and are the most common word used by the examiner
Prime Numbers are numbers that have exactly 2 factors: 1 and the number itself
Rational numbers are any whole number that can be written as a pure fraction or decimal, they must either end (terminating decimals) or repeat (recurring decimals)
Irrational numbers are any number that cannot be expressed in the form of a fraction, examples include π, √2, √7
Squared numbers are multiplying the number with itself, the square of the number is called a Perfect Square and is always positive
Cube numbers are multiplying the number with itself that number of times, the cube of the number is called a Perfect cube and may be positive or negative
Candidates are required to memorise square numbers up till 15 for Paper 1

definitions.definition: Numbers that have exactly 2 factors: 1 and the number itself
Numbers that have more than two factors
The largest number that is a common factor of two different numbers
The smallest number that is the multiple of two different numbers
The number obtained by interchanging the numerators and denominators of the original number

howToAnswer.instruction: Give the gradient value or equation directly with no working shown
Show clear substitution into gradient formula and calculate step by step
Draw accurate shape with key points marked and axes labelled
Draw both graphs accurately and read coordinates of intersection points

modelAnswer.question: A train travels from Lahore to Islamabad. The travel graph shows the journey. Find the speed of the train during the first 2 hours when it travels 160 km.
modelAnswer.answer: Speed = distance ÷ time. Distance = 160 km, Time = 2 hours. Speed = 160 ÷ 2 = 80 km/h
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Correct formula speed = distance ÷ time

commonMistakes: Including fractions or decimals in natural numbers when they are not included
Forgetting that integers is the most common word used by the examiner
Not recognizing that squared numbers are always positive while cube numbers may be positive or negative
Repeating the same element in a set when you are not allowed to do this
Confusing terminating decimals (which end) with recurring decimals (which repeat)
examinerTips: Integers is the most common word used by the examiner
Candidates are required to memorise square numbers up till 15 for Paper 1
A colon ':' should be read as 'Such that' in set notation
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Travel graph showing distance vs time with three sections: increasing line (moving away), horizontal line (stationary), and decreasing line (returning), with gradient calculations shown for each section
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (6 marks) — they asked students to find the gradient of a chord AB for the curve y = x² - 3, with points at x = 2 and x = h + 2, requiring use of the difference quotient formula
This topic appeared in O/N 2022 P1 Q8 (4 marks) — students had to find the gradient at x = π/3 for the curve y = 3tan(½x) - cos(2x) using differentiation of trigonometric functions

This topic was examined in M/J 2024 P2 Q6 (5 marks) — candidates needed to find the value of parameter a given that ln y against x has gradient 0.239 where y = 3^(2ax), testing logarithmic differentiation skills
This topic featured in O/N 2023 P1 Q7 (3 marks) — students were required to interpret gradient from a linearized graph involving exponential functions

workedExample.question: Find the gradient of the chord AB for the curve y = x² - 3, where the points are at x = 2 and x = h + 2. (4 marks)
steps: Step 1: Find coordinates of point A when x = 2: y = (2)² - 3 = 4 - 3 = 1, so A(2, 1)
Step 2: Find coordinates of point B when x = h + 2: y = (h + 2)² - 3 = h² + 4h + 4 - 3 = h² + 4h + 1, so B(h + 2, h² + 4h + 1)
Step 3: Apply gradient formula: gradient = (y₂ - y₁)/(x₂ - x₁)
Step 4: Substitute values: gradient = (h² + 4h + 1 - 1)/((h + 2) - 2) = (h² + 4h)/(h) = h(h + 4)/h = h + 4
workedExample.answer: The gradient of chord AB is h + 4
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for finding coordinates of A, 1 mark for coordinates of B, 1 mark for correct gradient formula, 1 mark for final simplified answer

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused gradient of chord with gradient of tangent' — always check whether the question asks for chord (between two points) or tangent (at one point) gradient
Examiner Report 2024: 'Students frequently made algebraic errors when simplifying the difference quotient' — always factorize the numerator completely before canceling with denominator terms
Examiner Report 2022: 'Candidates often forgot to substitute both x-coordinates correctly in the gradient formula' — ensure you identify both points clearly before applying (y₂-y₁)/(x₂-x₁)
note_data.keyDiagram: Draw a coordinate system showing a parabola (or given curve) with two distinct points A and B marked clearly. Draw a straight line connecting these points (the chord). Label the coordinates of both points, show the horizontal distance (x₂-x₁) and vertical distance (y₂-y₁) with dotted lines forming a right triangle. Include arrows indicating positive x and y directions, and annotate the gradient calculation alongside.
note_data.quickRecap: Numbers are classified into various families including natural numbers (positive integers), whole numbers (including zero), integers (positive, negative or zero), and rational/irrational numbers. Prime numbers have exactly 2 factors while composite numbers have more than two factors. Students must memorize square numbers up to 15 for Paper 1 and understand that integers is the most commonly used term by examiners.
